Footer.tagline: Intrface builds AI-native platforms, products, and systems.

hero.title: We build AI-native platforms, products, and systems.
hero.body: Intrface is a product-led studio building serious software with a platform mindset. Our flagship work spans terminal-first AI tooling, tourism exploration systems, and funding workflow platforms.

whatWeDo.label: What Intrface Does
whatWeDo.title: Product clarity, system leverage, and AI that behaves like real infrastructure.
whatWeDo.body: We are not presenting disconnected experiments. The portfolio is built around durable workflow design, reusable architecture, and product surfaces that communicate value quickly.

buildProducts.title: Build products
buildProducts.text: We design and ship product surfaces that explain themselves clearly and scale beyond a one-off prototype.

designSystems.title: Design systems
designSystems.text: We create reusable foundations for navigation, typography, interaction, and content architecture across a growing portfolio.

operationalizeAi.title: Operationalize AI
operationalizeAi.text: We turn AI from a feature into working infrastructure through agents, memory, orchestration, and knowledge-aware workflows.

platformLeverage.title: Create platform leverage
platformLeverage.text: We look for repeatable systems beneath each product so the portfolio becomes more coherent over time.

featuredProducts.label: Featured Products
featuredProducts.title: Three flagship products. One portfolio shaped by shared system thinking.
featuredProducts.body: AOC, Voyager, and Funda address different domains, but they all reflect the same approach: product truth first, strong workflow structure, and software built to carry operational complexity.

aoc.summary: A terminal-first AI workspace for context-aware development, memory, task orchestration, and unified operator flow.

voyager.summary: An AI-powered tourism platform that combines exploration, mapping, multilingual content, and multi-agent guidance.

funda.summary: A funding opportunity platform built around discovery, matching, collaboration, and AI-assisted application workflows.

capabilities.label: Shared Platform Capabilities
capabilities.title: We translate technical depth into product capability.
capabilities.body: The portfolio spans different industries, but common platform muscles appear throughout the work: memory, orchestration, permissions, realtime interaction, and knowledge-aware user experience.

whyIntrface.label: Why Intrface
whyIntrface.title: Not a collection of isolated apps — a coherent product and platform practice.
whyIntrface.body: What connects the work is a bias toward durable systems. We care about how products are operated, how information persists, how interfaces stay legible under complexity, and how infrastructure becomes a strategic advantage rather than hidden overhead.

publicProductTruth.label: Public product truth
publicProductTruth.text: AOC, Voyager, and Funda are grounded in active repositories, docs, and implementation detail rather than abstract portfolio copy.

systemThinking.label: System thinking
systemThinking.text: Across products, the same concerns recur: context, orchestration, role-aware flows, navigation, and durable operator experience.

staticFirstClarity.label: Static-first clarity
staticFirstClarity.text: The site is being built to explain real products with high signal, clean structure, and room for the portfolio to grow.

finalCta.label: Final CTA
finalCta.title: Explore the portfolio, review the flagship products, or start a meaningful conversation.
finalCta.body: Intrface is building a public-facing product ecosystem around real work. If you want to talk about a product, a collaboration, or the systems underneath them, this is the right place to start.

hero.label: Flagship Product
hero.title: A terminal-first AI workspace for agentic development.
hero.body: AOC unifies context-aware operation, project memory, integrated task management, and a Zellij-based workspace into one terminal environment for AI-assisted development.

painPoints.label: Why AOC
painPoints.title: Built for the real failure modes of AI-assisted development.
painPoints.body: AOC is grounded in a practical problem: most AI development workflows lose continuity fast. Context disappears, decisions go unrecorded, and work gets scattered across too many surfaces.
items: Lost context across sessions and tools
Manual copy-pasting between chats, notes, and code
No durable project memory for architectural decisions
Fragmented workflow across terminal, file manager, and task tracking

architecture.label: Distributed Cognitive Architecture
architecture.title: Three persistent project layers that give the workspace memory and continuity.
architecture.body: These are not abstract marketing labels. AOC persists project state in concrete repo artifacts so agents and operators can continue work across sessions with more context intact.

context.title: Context
context.description: Reactive project facts and structure maps that keep the workspace aware of the repo it is operating inside.

memory.title: Memory
memory.description: Persistent architectural memory that records decisions, constraints, and durable project knowledge over time.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.description: Dynamic work tracking with tags, subtasks, status, and terminal-native task operations for active execution.

workspace.label: Unified Workspace
workspace.title: One cockpit, organized around real terminal-native tools.
workspace.body: AOC brings together file navigation, agent operation, task management, and utility panes inside a persistent Zellij layout instead of pushing work across disconnected windows.

capabilities.label: Core Capabilities
capabilities.title: A rigorous operator surface, not a generic AI wrapper.
items: PI-only runtime with persistent project awareness
Native Taskmaster TUI for task and subtask management
RLM tooling for large codebase analysis
Yazi integration for file navigation inside the cockpit
Custom layouts and AOC modes for different operator needs
Alt+C control pane for configuration and optional integrations

quickStart.label: How It Works
quickStart.title: Install once, launch inside a project, continue with persistent state.
quickStart.body: The practical flow is straightforward: install AOC, verify the environment, run it inside a project, and operate from a cockpit that keeps context, memory, and tasks close to the work.

evolution.label: Roadmap / Evolution
evolution.title: AOC is evolving as a durable workspace system.
evolution.body: The trajectory is visible in the product itself: stronger workspace foundations, deeper continuity systems, better runtime control, and more robust workflows for serious coding projects.
items: Strengthen the workspace foundation around Zellij, PI, and Taskmaster
Deepen context and memory systems for longer-running projects
Refine the PI runtime and operator controls
Expand skills, layouts, and large-codebase workflows

finalCta.label: Final CTA
finalCta.title: Explore the repository, read the docs, or install AOC and work from the cockpit.
finalCta.body: AOC is already legible as a real product: a terminal-first AI workspace grounded in context, memory, tasks, and operator flow.

hero.label: Flagship Product
hero.title: An AI-powered tourism platform built for destinations, businesses, and travelers.
hero.body: Voyager combines multi-agent guidance, gamified exploration, multilingual content operations, and destination-scale platform infrastructure into one tourism system.

platform.label: What Voyager Is
platform.title: More than a travel app: a multi-tenant tourism platform with an exploration thesis.
platform.body: Voyager is built for a destination ecosystem, not only for end-user browsing. It connects agencies, businesses, attractions, and travelers in one platform where discovery, content, and AI assistance reinforce each other.

items.title: For destinations and agencies
items.text: Voyager gives destination operators a platform for managing tourism content, discovery flows, and region-level visitor guidance.

items.title: For businesses
items.text: Businesses gain entity-specific service context, operational visibility, and AI assistance tied to their own presence inside the destination system.

items.title: For travelers and scouts
items.text: Travel becomes exploratory rather than passive through map-led discovery, location-aware guidance, and immersive attraction interactions.

agents.label: Multi-Agent System
agents.title: Three domain-specific agents, each tied to a different tourism surface.
agents.body: Voyager’s agents are not generic chat wrappers. They are configured entities with their own role, knowledge, and route-aware context. Structured knowledge and RAG support the system behind the scenes.

items.summary: Regional guidance, itinerary support, and agency-level tourism intelligence for broad destination exploration.
Business-specific service context for menus, reservations, and localized visitor interaction.
Attraction-focused context, cultural storytelling, and gamified Scout interactions for meaningful on-site engagement.

scout.label: Scout Experience
scout.title: Travel as exploration, not just recommendation browsing.
scout.body: The Scout layer is one of Voyager’s clearest differentiators. It is built around discovery mechanics, location-aware interaction, and a device-like flow across map, compass, and camera modes.
items: Gamified exploration built around Scout discovery
Map, compass, and camera flows instead of static page browsing
Location-aware interactions that make place part of the product logic
A more meaningful travel experience than simple listings and recommendations

map.label: Map and Geospatial Layer
map.title: Discovery and navigation are native to the product model.
map.body: Voyager treats geography as platform structure. Mapped entities, route-aware interactions, and unified map presets all contribute to a more contextual tourism experience.
items: Unified map presets and geospatial configuration
Attractions, businesses, and agencies represented as mapped entities
Discovery and navigation workflows connected to user context
Location-aware routing and activation for map- and agent-driven journeys

operations.label: Content and Operations
operations.title: Multilingual content, ingestion pipelines, and role-aware platform control.
operations.body: Behind the user-facing exploration layer is a stronger operational system for tourism content, updates, and entity management across a destination ecosystem.
items: Multilingual content management with broad language coverage
Scraping and ingestion workflows for tourism content discovery
Role-aware operations for agencies, businesses, and admins
B2B2C structure connecting operators with end-user exploration

stakeholders.label: Platform Structure
stakeholders.title: A stakeholder model designed for the full tourism stack.
stakeholders.body: Voyager feels like a platform because it is organized around the relationships between multiple actors rather than a single user role.

proof.label: Product Proof
proof.title: Publicly legible signals from the implementation itself.

items.label: Multi-agent architecture
items.text: Voyager, Nexus, and Guardian are implemented as domain-specific agents with their own configuration, knowledge, and entity context.

items.label: Route-aware context switching
items.text: Agency, business, and attraction routes activate the relevant agent and keep chat behavior tied to entity context.

items.label: Knowledge-aware system
items.text: Scraped content is chunked, embedded, and retrieved through vector search so responses can draw from structured tourism information.

tech.label: Technical Architecture
tech.title: A concise systems summary for technical evaluators.
tech.body: The stack supports a mix of real-time operations, multilingual UX, AI orchestration, and knowledge-aware retrieval without reducing the product to implementation detail.
items: Convex realtime backend and vector search

finalCta.label: Final CTA
finalCta.title: Discuss destination deployment, partnership, or where Voyager fits in the wider Intrface portfolio.
finalCta.body: Voyager is best understood as a serious tourism platform with a distinct exploration layer, not as a generic travel interface. The next step is a real product conversation.

hero.label: Flagship Product
hero.title: A funding opportunity platform for discovery, matching, and application workflows.
hero.body: Funda connects organizations with funding opportunities through AI-assisted matching, role-aware workflows, and a structured application workspace.

platform.label: What Funda Is
platform.title: A role-based funding platform, not just a searchable list of grants.
platform.body: Funda is built around the operational reality of funding work: applicants, consultants, curators, and administrators all need different tools, views, and workflows inside the same system.

capabilities.label: Key Capabilities
capabilities.title: Funding workflows with stronger structure and better operational support.
capabilities.body: The product combines opportunity discovery with application execution, consultant collaboration, and deadline-oriented workspace behavior.
items: Application workspace for notes, documents, and checklists

system.label: Platform System
system.title: A funding product shaped around real coordination work.
system.body: Funda is not only about surfacing opportunities. It is also about supporting the messy middle: organizing documents, coordinating people, tracking progress, and keeping submission work moving.

tech.label: Technical Architecture
tech.title: A concise implementation snapshot.
tech.body: The current stack shows a serious product foundation with realtime data, AI assistance, role-aware auth, and multilingual routing already in place.

finalCta.label: Final CTA
finalCta.title: Discuss Funda as a product, a funding workflow system, or part of the wider Intrface portfolio.
finalCta.body: Funda extends the Intrface portfolio into structured funding operations: discovery, collaboration, and application execution in one platform.
